creator: pfg@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Thibault Jouan from comment #8)
>   About CMake, the hack, and hier, I think that maybe in my attempt
> for 0.16 I worked in this direction (with patches to CMake files and
> some python sources) and I think I complied to what was asked in
> porter's guide or other resources I checked at the end.

I looked at your changes but you were basically hardcoding the values, which is equally as bad. An option could have been to use REINPLACE to susbstitute strings but if there were a chance to set the value using CMAKE that would be better.

The other big lesson is to look upstream first and get the patches from them. That way, when you update the port to a newer version, it will be easier to just drop the patches.

I should say that if 0.17 is too unstable, perhaps it's better to clean up 0.16.
